PETITION Petition For An Order Directing Transfer Of Estate Property To Petitioner.
Off Calendar Nov-06-2009 Continued To Dec-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George McKray for Miguel Roliz (Successor Trustee).

Mr. McKray stated that Francisco left four children, that there was $100,000.00 left in life insurance, that he instructed his mother to care for his children, that the children challenged it and brought a lawsuit against their Grandmother, that their Grandmother stopped the litigation and gave the money to his children, and that because of the lawsuit they were disinherited as set forth on the record. In regard to the title of the real property, Mr. McKray stated that there was no specific title, that it simply stated "Genoveva as an unmarried woman", that the sellors were the Brothers of the Deceased, that there was a Trust set up; she didn't have the property at that time, and that she was living in the property under a life estate. In addition, he stated that there was a delay because they had to put the property in the Trust before they could sell it, that it was her intent to include the property, and that they do have a pourover will; everything goes to the Trust. Mr. McKray stated that he would do a petition as to the heirs and as to title. Upon the conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
